title: To His Nephew, To Be Prosperous In His Art Of Painting.
content:
On, as thou hast begun, brave youth, and get
    The palm from Urbin, Titian, Tintoret,
    Brugel and Coxu, and the works outdo
    Of Holbein and that mighty Rubens too.
    So draw and paint as none may do the like,
    No, not the glory of the world, Vandyke.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To His Nephew, To Be Prosperous In His Art Of Painting."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
